CREATE TABLE test_numeric (id INT PRIMARY KEY, num_value NUMERIC(10, 2));
SELECT AVG(num_value) FROM test_numeric WHERE num_value < 5000000000.00;
SELECT id, num_value FROM test_numeric WHERE num_value BETWEEN 1000.00 AND 5000.00;
CREATE TABLE test_related (id INT PRIMARY KEY, numeric_id INT REFERENCES test_numeric(id), description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_related (id, numeric_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'High Value');
INSERT INTO test_related (id, numeric_id, description) VALUES (2, 2, 'Negative Value');
INSERT INTO test_related (id, numeric_id, description) VALUES (3, 3, 'Zero Value');
SELECT a.id, a.num_value, b.description FROM test_numeric a JOIN test_related b ON a.id = b.numeric_id WHERE a.num_value > 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_numeric WHERE num_value IS NULL;
SELECT MIN(num_value) AS min_value FROM test_numeric;
SELECT MAX(num_value) AS max_value FROM test_numeric WHERE num_value < 1000000000.00;
UPDATE test_numeric SET num_value = num_value * 1.1 WHERE id = 1;
